report_name: Trump Mobile API Flaw Exposes Personal Data of 27,000 Smartphone Pre-Order Customers

threat_actors.name: Unidentified Australian Programmer
threat_actors.description: A self-taught Australian programmer who discovered and reported an unprotected API at Trump Mobile, accessing approximately 5,000 customer records to demonstrate the vulnerability before deleting the data.

attack_patterns.name: Vulnerability Scanning
attack_patterns.description: The researcher discovered the unprotected API endpoint through reconnaissance and testing of the Trump Mobile web application's API surface.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The core of the incident was the exploitation of a vulnerable, internet-facing API on the Trump Mobile website that did not require authentication to access customer records.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Exfiltration
attack_patterns.description: The researcher automated the process of querying the vulnerable API to download thousands of customer records in a short period by iterating through the dataset.

tools.name: cURL
tools.description: Mentioned as a likely command-line tool used to test and exploit the vulnerable API by sending HTTP POST requests.

vulnerabilities.name: Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA)
vulnerabilities.description: An API vulnerability, identified as OWASP API1:2023, where the server failed to verify if the user making a request was authorized to access the requested customer data records. This allowed an unauthenticated user to retrieve PII for 27,000 customers.

identities.name: Trump Mobile
identities.description: A smartphone company whose pre-order website had an unprotected API, leading to the exposure of personal information for approximately 27,000 customers of its T1 smartphone.

infrastructure.name: Trump Mobile Pre-order API Endpoint
infrastructure.description: An unprotected and publicly accessible API endpoint on the Trump Mobile website that allowed unauthenticated access to customer pre-order records.

indicators.name: Suspicious API Endpoint Access Patterns
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious API Endpoint Access Patterns
id: 9b2d5c1a-7e8f-4b1a-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects access to common API endpoint patterns that may be indicative of reconnaissance or exploitation attempts against user or order data.
references:
  - https://www.ghacks.net/2026/05/24/researcher-claims-trump-mobile-website-leaked-data-on-more-than-27000-customers-through-unprotected-api/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-24
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|re:
      - '/api/v[1-9]/(users|customers|accounts)'
      - '/api/v[1-9]/(orders|preorders)'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects web requests to common API URL patterns used for accessing user or order data, which could indicate scanning for or exploitation of insecure APIs.

indicators.name: Potential API Enumeration via cURL
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential API Enumeration via cURL
id: 3e4f5a6b-7c8d-4b1a-9e8f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of cURL to send POST requests to API endpoints, which can be used to test for and exploit API vulnerabilities like BOLA.
references:
  - https://www.ghacks.net/2026/05/24/researcher-claims-trump-mobile-website-leaked-data-on-more-than-27000-customers-through-unprotected-api/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-24
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1020
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\curl.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '-X POST'
      - '/api/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects command-line execution of cURL sending a POST request to an API endpoint, a common method for exploiting insecure APIs for data exfiltration.
